criteria.label: Open, run and share a Colab notebook with no local setup.
criteria.example: `File → New notebook` in Colab gives you Python 3 + most ML libs preinstalled. Share via `File → Share` (Google Drive perms) or a public link; collaborators run it in their browser, no install.

criteria.label: Select a GPU/TPU runtime and verify the accelerator.
criteria.example: `Runtime → Change runtime type → T4 GPU`, then verify: `!nvidia-smi` shows the GPU; `import torch; torch.cuda.is_available()` returns True. Without selecting, you silently get a CPU runtime.

criteria.label: Mount Drive and use Colab Secrets to persist data and protect keys.
criteria.example:
```python
from google.colab import drive, userdata
drive.mount('/content/drive')              # data survives disconnects
import os
os.environ['OPENAI_API_KEY'] = userdata.get('OPENAI_API_KEY')  # not in the notebook
```

criteria.label: Design around the ephemeral runtime (re-install, checkpoint, timeouts).
criteria.example: Setup cell at top: `%pip install -q langchain && drive.mount(...)`. Training loop: checkpoint to `/content/drive/MyDrive/ckpt/` every N steps so a disconnect after 4 hours doesn't lose the run.
